concerns.text: Acceptance Scenario 3 requires confirming 'no correlation with intermediate activation maps' but does not specify the statistical test, threshold, or sampling method to verify this independence. 'No correlation' is a binary state that requires a defined metric (e.g., Pearson r < 0.1) to be verifiable.

Acceptance Scenario 1 states the model must operate at 'reduced rank (≤ 50% of max capacity)' but does not define 'max capacity' numerically (e.g., rank 64 vs 128). Without a concrete definition of the baseline rank, the 50% threshold is unverifiable.

Acceptance Scenario 1 claims latency reduction '≥ 30% compared to the static 0.2B baseline' but the baseline itself is not defined as a specific, reproducible configuration (e.g., specific batch size, input resolution, or CPU frequency scaling state). The comparison is ambiguous without a fixed baseline spec.

FR-007 requires ensuring evaluation targets are 'mathematically independent' of gating inputs. This is a theoretical property, not a functional test. The spec needs a verifiable procedure (e.g., 'Run a permutation test on gating inputs; p-value > 0.05') to confirm this independence empirically.

SC-005 sets a target of '30-40% latency reduction' but does not specify the confidence interval or statistical power required to claim this result. A single run showing 31% reduction is not a verifiable success criterion without a defined statistical validation protocol.
